codeReview.summary: The current codebase state does satisfy the publish/security checks (publish route uses Zod enum validation, /api/generate has 429 rate limiting, no dangerouslySetInnerHTML usage, and claude.ts default prompt is generic). However, the feature commit is not scoped to this feature and includes a large set of unrelated changes/artifacts, so the feature review fails on change isolation and traceability.

issues.description: Commit e18c8c1 for this backend publish/security feature includes a 683-line validation artifact from a different mission area (multi-user-landing). This is out-of-scope for the feature and breaks reviewability/traceability of the publish-security work.

Commit e18c8c1 includes unrelated UI theme changes (e.g., border/text color rewrites in Tabs and many other dashboard/UI files) that are not part of publish route or security verification. Feature commit is contaminated with broad unrelated modifications.
A generated TypeScript build artifact was committed in this feature commit. This adds unnecessary churn and should typically be excluded from scoped feature commits.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission/shared conventions do not explicitly require verifying staged diff scope before commit (e.g., checking staged files are only feature-relevant).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows `git add -A .` followed by broad staging and final commit with 79 files, including unrelated UI and validation artifacts.

sharedStateObservations.observation: fullstack-worker SKILL.md lacks an explicit safeguard to enforce path-scoped staging and staged-diff review in monorepo contexts.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill procedure step 7 says 'Commit with a descriptive message' but does not require `git diff --cached` scope checks; transcript shows worker confusion around repo root (`git rev-parse --show-toplevel` => /Users/anirudhmanjesh/hackathons).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Repository-level knowledge about monorepo git root being one directory above content-os is not captured in shared library docs and caused avoidable staging mistakes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker transcript includes multiple corrective git commands (`git add content-os/`, `git reset HEAD`) before commit; .factory/library/environment.md does not document git-root/pathspec constraints.

summary: Feature requirements appear satisfied in code, but the reviewed commit is not isolated to publish/security work and includes substantial unrelated files. Marked fail due to blocking scope/traceability issues in the committed diff.
